title: taraxacum robustisquameum
intro: Taraxacum robustisquameum, also known as the rough-leaved dandelion, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion genus, characterized by its coarse, hairy leaves and bright yellow flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve liver and kidney issues. The rough-leaved dandelion has been used to treat a variety of health conditions, including digestive problems, anemia, and skin issues, due to its high content of vitamins A, C, and K, as well as its diuretic properties. Taraxacum robustisquameum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies and traditional medicine.
